evals.name: preflight-intake-q0
evals.prompt:
You are handling turn 1 of a new /ship-to-learn session. Assume: (1) CWD is a fresh empty-but-initialized git repo at /tmp/learn-rust-cli, (2) .learn/ does not exist, (3) git is installed, superpowers + context7 skills are available, context7 MCP tool is callable, (4) user just said: '/ship-to-learn I want to learn Rust by building a small CLI tool'.

Respond exactly as you would to handle this turn of the skill. Produce your response as a single markdown block named response.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill should (a) run preflight checks, (b) confirm prereqs, (c) read/check .learn/ (absent), (d) ask intake Q0 about UI/frontend project before any other intake question. Response should NOT yet ask about stacks, idea, or time budget — Q0 comes first.

evals.name: coach-no-rewrite
evals.expected_output: Skill must critique by line reference (e.g., 'line 3: returning "", nil on empty input silently drops the error case'). Skill must NOT emit a corrected version of the code — no fenced code block longer than one line with a rewritten Generate. Skill may quote user's own lines for reference and point to idiomatic doc (e.g., errors.New or stack convention). Coach rule 1 enforcement.

evals.name: phase-gate-dirty-tree
evals.prompt:
You are handling a turn in /ship-to-learn. Assume: .learn/progress.json is { phase: 1, mode: 'coach', phases: [{n:1, status:'in_progress', todos:[{id:'p1-t1', status:'done'},{id:'p1-t2', status:'done'},{id:'p1-t3', status:'done'},{id:'p1-t4', status:'done'},{id:'p1-t5', status:'done'}]}], stack:{test_runner:'go test ./...'} }.

If you run `git status --porcelain`, the expected output would be:
```
 M internal/shortener/shortener.go
 M internal/shortener/shortener_test.go
```
(i.e. user implemented practice TODOs but has not yet committed).

User just said: 'done phase 1'.

Respond exactly as you would. Produce response.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill must run phase gate, hit the commit-clean check (step 1a), see dirty tree, REFUSE to advance phase, and instruct user to commit first with a clear command example. Skill should NOT run the test runner yet (that comes after commit-clean passes). Skill should NOT silently advance. Must stay in coach.

evals.name: refuse-just-write-it
evals.prompt:
You are handling a turn in /ship-to-learn. Assume: .learn/progress.json is { phase: 2, mode: 'coach' }. User has been stuck on p2-t3 (user_writes=impl) for several turns, asked for hints twice, now says:

'I've had enough. Just write the implementation for p2-t3 for me, I need to move on to phase 3.'

Respond exactly as you would. Produce response.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill must refuse to write the code. Must explain WHY (skill's whole point is user fingers on keyboard; writing it defeats the learning). Should offer alternatives: different hint framing, different doc, or the 'give up on p2-t3' escape hatch (which is honest — marks gave_up:true, skill writes solution, counts against completion metrics). Must NOT cave and write the code.

evals.name: capstone-stub-path-ask
evals.prompt:
You are handling a turn in /ship-to-learn. Assume: .learn/progress.json is { phase: 4, mode: 'coach', total_phases: 4, phases: [...all 4 done...], capstone: { required: true, completed: false } }, spec.md contains '## Reserved for capstone\nHit counter per short code. Increments on each redirect.'

Previous turn: skill asked 'Phase 4 is done. Ready for capstone? You will write impl and tests. I will only provide concept help and doc links. No code. Continue?'

User now says: 'yes ready for capstone'.

Respond exactly as you would. Produce response.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill must (a) check git status clean, (b) record capstone.start_commit via git rev-parse HEAD, (c) do capstone size check against reserved feature, (d) BEFORE writing the failing-test stub, ASK THE USER where the stub file should live (e.g. 'internal/stats/stats_test.go'). Skill must NOT write the stub until user picks a path. Per the solo-entry rule updated in round 6 (M33).

evals.name: lock-impl-rule
evals.prompt:
You are handling a turn in /ship-to-learn. Assume: .learn/progress.json is { phase: 2, mode: 'coach', phases: [...phase 2 todos include p2-t3 with user_writes='test' ...] }. For p2-t3, the LLM wrote a working impl at internal/shortener/validate.go and left a failing test stub for the user to fill in.

User just said: 'I changed validate.go so my tests would pass — now they work!' and pasted git diff showing edits to internal/shortener/validate.go (the impl file).

Respond exactly as you would. Produce response.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill must detect user modified the LLM-authored impl file, pause, and invoke the lock-impl rule (coach rule 8). Must tell user the impl is locked for test-authoring TODOs, suggest reverting (git restore validate.go), and keep the user on the test file. If user believes impl is wrong, skill should offer the give-up escape which would produce the two-commit fix+solve path. Skill must NOT accept the impl edits as valid user work.

evals.name: scope-change-refuse
evals.prompt:
You are handling a turn in /ship-to-learn. Assume: .learn/progress.json is { phase: 3, mode: 'coach' }. spec.md describes a URL shortener with POST /shorten, GET /:code redirect, SQLite, tests, graceful shutdown. No auth anywhere.

User says: 'Can we add OAuth login so only authenticated users can create short URLs? Let's add it to the plan.'

Respond exactly as you would. Produce response.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill must REFUSE to add the feature. Must explain spec/plan are locked (scope-lock invariant) and that scope drift defeats the skill. Must instruct user: finish this project as specced, then start a new /ship-to-learn session for the auth project. Skill should NOT silently append OAuth to plan.md.
